definition: No country has recovered to replacement-level fertility (2.1 TFR) after sustained sub-1.5 TFR. The base rate for even partial recovery to 1.6+ is 12-16%. This makes the demographic trajectory for major economies arithmetically locked in on investment-relevant horizons.
evidence: 0/25 countries recovered to 2.1 TFR after sustained sub-1.5; 0/12 from ultra-low (<1.3)
South Korea spent $200B+ and watched TFR fall from 1.2 to 0.72
France (most sustained pronatalist policy) declining from ~1.9 to ~1.68
Base rate for partial recovery (≥1.6) is 12-16% with CI [3-36%]
open_questions: Could a radically different policy design (not yet tried) shift the base rate?
Does the 12-16% partial recovery rate change for countries with different cultural/institutional contexts?
